pois.name: Völklinger Hütte
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site and former ironworks, this massive complex is the only industrial plant of its kind preserved in its entirety from the 19th and 20th centuries.

pois.name: Aussichtspunkt Cloef (Saarschleife)
pois.description: The most famous landmark of Saarland, offering a panoramic view of the 180-degree bend of the Saar River carved into the Taunus quartzite rock.

pois.name: Ludwigskirche
pois.description: A Protestant baroque church in Saarbrücken, considered one of the most important baroque church buildings in Germany, designed by Friedrich Joachim Stengel.

pois.name: Burg Kirkel
pois.description: A medieval castle ruin situated on a high rock, featuring a prominent round keep and hosting a popular medieval summer festival and craft workshop.

pois.name: Schlossberghöhlen Homburg
pois.description: Europe's largest man-made bunter sandstone caves, spanning twelve floors with impressive yellow and red sand formations originally created for sand mining.

pois.name: Abtei Tholey
pois.description: The oldest monastery on German soil, this Benedictine abbey features modern stained-glass windows designed by Gerhard Richter and an early Gothic abbey church.

pois.name: Römische Villa Borg
pois.description: A meticulously reconstructed Roman villa rustica that offers a vivid insight into rural Roman life, including functioning baths and a traditional tavern.

pois.name: Wendelinusbasilika
pois.description: A late Gothic hall church in St. Wendel containing the remains of Saint Wendelin, featuring intricate stone carvings and 15th-century frescoes.

pois.name: Der Gollenstein
pois.description: One of the tallest menhirs in Central Europe, standing 6.58 meters high. This Neolithic stone monument is approximately 4,000 years old.

pois.name: Saarbrücker Schloss
pois.description: A baroque palace complex with a modern glass center pavillion, sitting above medieval casemates that can be explored underground.

pois.name: Burg Montclair
pois.description: Strategic medieval ruins located on the ridge of the Saarschleife, once part of a massive defensive system overlooking the river bend.

pois.name: Teufelsburg
pois.description: A reconstructed rock castle in Überherrn providing extensive views across the Saarlouis basin and into neighboring France.

pois.name: Europadenkmal Berus
pois.description: A monument dedicated to the founders of Europe, Robert Schuman, Konrad Adenauer, and Alcide de Gasperi, located on the French-German border.

pois.name: Brennender Berg
pois.description: A smoldering coal seam that has been burning since 1668. Johann Wolfgang von Goethe famously visited this geologic curiosity in 1770.

pois.name: Ottweiler Altstadt
pois.description: A historic town center featuring a 'leaning' tower, winding alleys, and many half-timbered houses from the 16th to 18th centuries.

pois.name: Bostalsee
pois.description: The largest recreational lake in southwest Germany, created in the 1970s as part of the Saar-Hunsrück Nature Park.

pois.name: Römermuseum Schwarzenacker
pois.description: An open-air museum displaying the excavated foundations of a Gallo-Roman vicus, including reconstructed streets and a baroque garden.

pois.name: Alte Brücke Saarbrücken
pois.description: Originally built by Charles V, this is the oldest surviving bridge in Saarland, connecting the districts of Saarbrücken and St. Johann.

pois.name: Keltenring Otzenhausen
pois.description: One of the most impressive Celtic hillforts in Europe, featuring massive stone walls reaching up to 10 meters in height.

pois.name: Schaumbergturm
pois.description: A lookout tower on the Schaumberg hill, Saarland's 'Hausberg', offering views as far as the Vosges mountains in France on clear days.

pois.name: Burg Kerpen
pois.description: Ruins of a medieval spur castle in Illingen, once the seat of the Knights of Kerpen, now featuring an accessible residential tower.

pois.name: Blieskastel Altstadt
pois.description: A well-preserved baroque town center and former residence of the Counts of von der Leyen, known for its elegant townhouses and the 'Orangerie'.

pois.name: Klosterruine Wörschweiler
pois.description: Atmospheric ruins of a former Cistercian monastery located on a high hill, demonstrating the transition from Romanesque to Gothic architecture.

pois.name: Schloss Dagstuhl
pois.description: A manor house now serving as an international informatics center, adjacent to the ruins of a much older medieval hilltop fortress.

pois.name: Litermont-Gipfel
pois.description: A volcanic rock summit associated with the legend of the Knight Maldix, featuring a summit cross and panoramic views of the Prims valley.

pois.name: Peterbergkapelle
pois.description: A traditional pilgrimage chapel dedicated to St. Peter on one of the highest elevations in northern Saarland.

pois.name: Losheimer See
pois.description: A large reservoir and popular recreation spot known for its circular hiking trail and the surrounding 'Garden of the Senses'.

pois.name: Burg Siersberg
pois.description: Medieval ruins perched above the Nied river, offering strategic views toward the Saarlouis fortification and the French border.

pois.name: Alter Turm Mettlach
pois.description: The oldest religious building in Saarland, this octagonal funerary chapel was part of the former Benedictine abbey of Mettlach.

pois.name: Schloss Münchweiler
pois.description: A late baroque palace complex built for the von Zandt family, surrounded by a large park and featuring well-preserved historic interiors.

pois.name: Spicherer Höhen
pois.description: A historic battlefield from the Franco-Prussian War (1870) and WWII, featuring several monuments and remains of fortifications.

pois.name: Grube Reden
pois.description: A former coal mine transformed into a cultural and landscape park, featuring the 'Gondwana' dinosaur museum and water gardens.

pois.name: Sankt Johanner Markt
pois.description: The heart of Saarbrücken's life, this square is surrounded by baroque townhouses, cafes, and the Stengel-designed market fountain.

pois.name: Brauerei Bruch
pois.description: The oldest brewery in Saarland, established in 1702, representing the long-standing brewing tradition of the region.

pois.name: Burg Nohfelden
pois.description: A castle ruin with a prominent 20-meter tall keep that is open to the public, offering views over the Nahe river valley.

pois.name: Valentinskapelle Düren
pois.description: A small late Gothic chapel known for its peaceful location and historic significance as a local pilgrimage site.

pois.name: B-Werk Besseringen
pois.description: The only completely preserved fortification of the 'Westwall' (Siegfried Line) that can be toured in its original state.

pois.name: Fellenbergmühle
pois.description: A historic mill and workshop museum in Merzig that demonstrates traditional fine precision mechanics and toolmaking.

pois.name: Historisches Gasthaus Stiefel
pois.description: One of the city's oldest traditional inns, located at the St. Johanner Markt, reflecting the typical bourgeois life of the 18th century.

pois.name: Schloss Karlsberg Ruine
pois.description: Foundation remains of what was once one of the largest baroque palace complexes in Europe, destroyed by French revolutionary troops.

pois.name: Burg Bucherbach
pois.description: A water castle ruin in Köllerbach with significant remaining walls and a moat, illustrating the medieval defense of the Saar basin.

pois.name: Odilienkapelle
pois.description: A small chapel near a healing spring dedicated to Saint Odilia, located in a quiet forest setting near Blieskastel.

pois.name: Glashütte Wadgassen
pois.description: The site of a major former glassworks, now housing the Villeroy & Boch outlet and a museum of glass technology and design.

pois.name: Sudelfels
pois.description: A striking dolomite rock formation in the Saargau region, once the site of a Gallo-Roman cult center.

pois.name: Maria im Walde
pois.description: A forest chapel in Ensdorf established as a place of prayer for miners, known locally for its tranquil atmosphere.

pois.name: Schloss Halberg
pois.description: A Neo-Gothic palace located on a wooded hill, currently the headquarters of the Saarländischer Rundfunk broadcasting company.

pois.name: Hieburg
pois.description: Hidden ruins of a small medieval castle located in the forest near Nonnweiler-Kastel.

pois.name: Keltenpark Otzenhausen
pois.description: A reconstructed Celtic village at the foot of the Hunnenring fortress, showcasing authentic living conditions and crafts of the Treveri tribe.

pois.name: Wolfskuhl
pois.description: A historic site and nature trail near St. Ingbert associated with old legends of mining and local folklore.

pois.name: Evangelische Kirche Mettlach
pois.description: A Neo-Gothic church built by the Boch family, featuring elegant terracotta decorations from the family factory.

pois.name: Stummsche Kapelle
pois.description: A funerary chapel built for the industrialist Stumm family in Neunkirchen, displaying rich Victorian-style stonework.

pois.name: Grube Velsen
pois.description: A visitor mine showcasing authentic mining machinery and the daily life of miners in the Saar district during the peak coal era.

pois.name: Alte Schmelz
pois.description: One of Germany's oldest industrial sites, this ironworks in St. Ingbert features the historic 'Möllerhalle' and worker's housing.

pois.name: Großer Stiefel
pois.description: A bizarrely shaped sandstone rock resembling a boot, likely used as a cult site by early Germanic or Celtic inhabitants.

pois.name: Hindenburgturm Bexbach
pois.description: A museum and lookout tower in Bexbach, focusing on the history of the region's mining and the Saar referendum.

pois.name: Burg Liebenstein
pois.description: The modest ruins of a 13th-century castle in Neunkirchen-Wellesweiler, which once guarded the transport routes.

pois.name: Maria Trost Kapelle
pois.description: A notable modern pilgrimage chapel in Ensdorf, built after WWII on the site of previous religious shrines.

pois.name: Wendelinushof
pois.description: A historic farmstead once belonging to the Tholey Abbey, now serving as a model for ecological agriculture and local tradition.

pois.name: Karlsberg Brauerei
pois.description: One of the largest breweries in Germany, located in Homburg and named after the nearby Karlsberg Castle ruins.

pois.name: Steinerne Brücke Oberkirchen
pois.description: An impressive multi-arched stone bridge over the Ostertal valley, built during the era of French administration.
